Home / Projects / Puyallup DSHS Building
Project Type Office Remodel
Location Puyallup, WA
Sector Government / Office

Puyallup DSHS Building

Government office facilities demand a unique balance of functionality, durability, and accessibility. The Puyallup DSHS Building remodel project required us to modernize an existing office space while meeting stringent government standards for safety, accessibility, and operational efficiency.

Working within an occupied building that serves the public daily, we coordinated construction activities to minimize disruption to essential government services. Our team managed comprehensive interior renovations including new partition systems, updated HVAC and electrical infrastructure, accessibility compliance upgrades, and commercial-grade finishes designed for high-traffic government use.

The project exemplifies our experience working with government agencies and understanding the regulatory requirements that govern public facilities. From ADA compliance to specific security considerations, we navigated complex requirements while delivering a modern, functional office environment.

Project Highlights

  • Comprehensive office remodel meeting government standards
  • Enhanced accessibility features and ADA compliance throughout
  • Upgraded mechanical and electrical systems for efficiency
  • Phased construction minimizing disruption to ongoing operations
  • Durable commercial finishes suited for high-traffic public use
  • Coordination with multiple stakeholders and regulatory requirements

The Puyallup DSHS Building project demonstrates Northway's capability to handle sensitive remodel work in occupied facilities, delivering quality results while respecting operational continuity and stringent regulatory compliance.

Need an Office Remodel or Renovation?

We specialize in remodeling occupied facilities with minimal disruption to your operations.
